message.content:
Run CODEX (GPT-5.6) to gate the config-conflict collapse. Write /tmp/cfg_gate.md then 'cd /tmp && timeout 240 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check - < /tmp/cfg_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -30' (embed inline; medium effort; no sandbox flags).
Context: 20 PG_ keys had conflicting fallback defaults. We collapsed the BYTE-SAFE (env-set: env wins, only unused fallback changed) and SAFE-PRIMARY (primary-path value kept, secondary aligned up, no faithfulness weakening) keys; DEFERRED the rest. Investigation: [{"key":"PG_AGENTIC_MAX_ROUNDS","values_seen":["12","2"],"env_set":true,"env_value":"15","chosen_value":"15","classification":"BYTE-SAFE","reason":"Set in .env=15; os.getenv returns 15 at runtime regardless of the '12'/'2' hardcoded fallbacks. Aligning all code defaults to 15 is byte-identical."},{"key":"PG_ANALYSIS_BATCH_TIMEOUT","values_seen":["180","240.0"],"env_set":true,"env_value":"900.0","chosen_value":"900.0","classification":"BYTE-SAFE","reason":"Set in .env=900.0; env wins at runtime over both fallbacks. Aligning defaults to 900.0 is byte-identical."},{"key":"PG_ANALYSIS_PIPELINE","values_seen":["","8phase"],"env_set":false,"env_value":null,"chosen_value":"8phase","classification":"SAFE-PRIMARY","reason":"Not in .env. Both call sites in react_agent.py resolve to '8phase' when unset: line 51 default='8phase'; line 674 uses '' then 'or "8phase"' = '8phase'. The '' is not a real behavioral conflict. Collapse to '8phase'."},{"key":"PG_CLUSTER_BATCH_TIMEOUT","values_seen":["300","600"],"env_set":true,"env_value":"600","chosen_value":"600","classification":"BYTE-SAFE","reason":"Set in .env=600; env wins. Aligning defaults to 600 is byte-identical."},{"key":"PG_CONTENT_PER_SOURCE","values_seen":["10000","25000"],"env_set":true,"env_value":"25000","chosen_value":"25000","classification":"BYTE-SAFE","reason":"Set in .env=25000; env wins over both fallbacks. Byte-identical."},{"key":"PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD","values_seen":["0.65","0.75"],"env_set":true,"env_value":"0.75","chosen_value":"0.75","classification":"BYTE-SAFE","reason":"FAITHFULNESS-CRITICAL but env decides: .env=0.75 (the STRICTER of 0.65/0.75). env wins at runtime, so aligning defaults to 0.75 is byte-identical and does not weaken faithfulness (it raises the weak path to the strict value)."},{"key":"PG_FETCH_CONCURRENCY","values_seen":["10","5"],"env_set":true,"env_value":"10","chosen_value":"10","classification":"BYTE-SAFE","reason":"Set in .env=10; env wins over the 10/5 fallbacks. Byte-identical."},{"key":"PG_FETCH_TIMEOUT","values_seen":["20","30"],"env_set":false,"env_value":null,"chosen_value":"30","classification":"SAFE-PRIMARY","reason":"Not in .env. graph_v2.py:63 FETCH_TIMEOUT=20 is DEAD (assigned at module scope, referenced nowhere; graph_v2 not imported as a live pipeline). fetch_limiter.py:34 FETCH_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=30 is the live path: actively used in rate_limited_fetch (lines 82/87/94) and exported via retrieval/__init__.py. Primary value=30."},{"key":"PG_GENERATOR_MODEL","values_seen":["","<none>"],"env_set":true,"env_value":"z-ai/glm-5.2","chosen_value":"z-ai/glm-5.2","classification":"BYTE-SAFE","reason":"Set in .env=z-ai/glm-5.2; env wins over the ''/no-default fallbacks. Byte-identical at runtime."},{"key":"PG_MAX_CITATION_FREQUENCY","values_seen":["5","8"],"env_set":true,"env_value":"10","chosen_value":"10","classification":"BYTE-SAFE","reason":"Set in .env=10; env wins over the 5/8 fallbacks. Byte-identical (note runtime is 10, neither seen default)."},{"key":"PG_MAX_CROSS_SOURCE_PAIRS","values_seen":["200","50"],"env_set":true,"env_value":"50","chosen_value":"50","classification":"BYTE-SAFE","reason":"Set in .env=50; env wins. Byte-identical."},{"key":"PG_MAX_TOTAL_ACADEMIC","values_seen":["100","500"],"env_set":true,"env_value":"100","chosen_value":"100","classification":"BYTE-SAFE","reason":"Set in .env=100; env wins over 100/500 fallbacks. Byte-identical."},{"key":"PG_MAX_WORDS_PER_SECTION","values_seen":["1500","2000"],"env_set":true,"env_value":"3000","chosen_value":"3000","classification":"BYTE-SAFE","reason":"Set in .env=3000; env wins over the 1500/2000 fallbacks. Byte-identical (runtime is 3000, neither seen default). Raising a MAX cap does not weaken any evidence/faithfulness floor."},{"key":"PG_MIN_EVIDENCE_PER_SECTION","values_seen":["3","5","8"],"env_set":true,"env_value":"5","chosen_value":"5","classification":"BYTE-SAFE","reason":"FAITHFULNESS-CRITICAL but env decides: .env=5. env wins at runtime on every path, so aligning all three fallbacks (3/5/8) to 5 is byte-identical. Per faithfulness rule, lowering an evidence floor is allowed ONLY when env explicitly sets the lower value — here env explicitly sets 5, so collapsing the 8-default path down to 5 reflects the already-authoritative runtime value (no behavior change)."},{"key":"PG_MIN_EVIDENCE_UTILIZATION","values_seen":["0.30","0.40"],"env_set":true,"env_value":"0.40","chosen_value":"0.40","classification":"BYTE-SAFE","reason":"FAITHFULNESS-CRITICAL but env decides: .env=0.40 (the STRICTER of 0.30/0.40). env wins, so aligning defaults to 0.40 is byte-identical and strengthens (not weakens) the weak 0.30 path."},{"key":"PG_MIN_QUOTE_WORDS","values_seen":["15","5"],"env_set":true,"env_value":"5","chosen_value":"5","classification":"BYTE-SAFE","reason":"Set in .env=5; env wins over 15/5 fallbacks. Byte-identical. Quote-length knob, not an evidence/verification floor."},{"key":"PG_MIN_TOTAL_WORDS","values_seen":["0","10000"],"env_set":true,"env_value":"0","chosen_value":"0","classification":"BYTE-SAFE","reason":"Set in .env=0; env wins over the 0/10000 fallbacks. Byte-identical. This is a report-length floor, not a faithfulness/evidence gate; env already disables it at runtime, so collapsing defaults to 0 changes no runtime behavior."},{"key":"PG_REACT_INTERPRET_TIMEOUT","values_seen":["240","180"],"env_set":false,"env_value":null,"chosen_value":"180","classification":"SAFE-PRIMARY","reason":"Not in .env. react_agent.py:48 module constant _INTERPRET_TIMEOUT=240 is DEAD (referenced nowhere). The two live runtime reads are inline: line 3559 in _write_interpretation (8phase write path) and line 8164 in _interpret_results, both default=180. Primary value=180."},{"key":"PG_REDUNDANCY_JACCARD_THRESHOLD","values_seen":["0.45","0.65"],"env_set":true,"env_value":"0.65","chosen_value":"0.65","classification":"BYTE-SAFE","reason":"Set in .env=0.65; env wins over the 0.45/0.65 fallbacks. Byte-identical. Dedup threshold, not a faithfulness floor."},{"key":"PG_TARGET_TOTAL_WORDS","values_seen":["12000","8000"],"env_set":true,"env_value":"8000","chosen_value":"8000","classification":"BYTE-SAFE","reason":"Set in .env=8000; env wins over the 12000/8000 fallbacks. Byte-identical. Report-length target, not a faithfulness gate."}]. Collapse: {"keys_collapsed":19,"keys_deferred":["PG_GENERATOR_MODEL"],"files_changed":12,"collection_after":"16738 tests collected, 11 errors (matches baseline 16738/11 exactly)"}. Verify: {"oracle_matches":true,"oracle_sha":"9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98","collection_ok":true,"characterization_ok":true,"notes":"CONCLUSION: config-conflict collapse in /home/polaris/wt/phase-cfg is SCORE-SAFE. Details:

ORACLE (matches=true, sha=9c0a3d43...): The literal replay command in the task FAILS in phase-cfg with "3 recorded request(s) never replayed in acceptance_retrieval.jsonl ... First: run_live_retrieval call_id='0'" — but this is NOT a score regression. Two provisioning issues in the phase-cfg worktree had to be worked through:
  (a) The cassettes dir (tests/oracle/cassettes/) was EMPTY in phase-cfg. I copied acceptance_golden.json / acceptance_llm.jsonl / acceptance_retrieval.jsonl from a sibling worktree (phase0) — they are byte-identical (sha256) across all 5 worktrees checked, and acceptance_golden.json's own sha256 IS 9c0a3d43...9db98, i.e. the expected oracle SHA. (I removed these copies afterward; the worktree cassettes dir is empty again as found.)
  (b) Even with cassettes present the replay fails, because phase-cfg's TRACKED oracle harness (tests/oracle/llm_cassette.py + cassette.py, committed at HEAD c5448ec) is an OLDER version than phase0's (HEAD 7adea84, commit 8eec65c "make acceptance harness trustworthy"). phase0's harness freezes BOTH OpenRouterClient.generate AND generate_structured; phase-cfg's stale harness only freezes generate, leaving the decide-step generate_structured LLM call unfrozen. That non-determinism changes the outline agent's control flow and hence how many run_live_retrieval calls fire, so 3 retrieval entries recorded by the newer harness go unreplayed. The retrieval-cassette failure is a downstream HARNESS-VERSION artifact, independent of source code.

PROOF it is not the collapse: I git-stashed the working-tree collapse diff (12 src files) so phase-cfg sat at its committed byte-identical config-migration state; the replay failed IDENTICALLY (same "3 never replayed"). Restored the stash — working tree is back to the 12 modified files exactly as found. Meanwhile clean phase0 replays BYTE-IDENTICAL to golden: "[oracle] GOLDEN SHA-256: 9c0a3d43...9db98" + "ACCEPTANCE PASSED: all run controls valid". So: collapse working-tree change did NOT introduce the failure (committed baseline fails the same way), and the correct harness (phase0) yields the expected SHA + passing controls.

Diff review: committed phase-cfg vs phase0 on the outline/retrieval path is pure os.getenv("KEY","default") -> resolve("KEY") substitution plus the settings import — the only non-getenv changed line is a "+import"; no control-flow/behavioral change. Consistent with the branch's "byte-identical" commit messages.

I did NOT overwrite phase-cfg's harness with phase0's — the auto-mode classifier correctly blocked that as out-of-scope for a read-only verification task, and I honored it. NOTE FOR OPERATOR: phase-cfg should have its untracked/tracked oracle harness (llm_cassette.py, cassette.py) refreshed to the phase0 version and its cassettes populated before its replay can pass locally; this is a worktree-provisioning fix, not a code fix.

COLLECTION (ok=true): No code changes made in this thread (worktree left exactly as found — cassettes copies removed, stash popped, harness untouched). Pre-established 16738 collected / 11 errors reference carried through unchanged per task instruction; env not re-run for collection here.

CHARACTERIZATION (ok=true): '/home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_config_registry.py tests/test_settings_models.py -q' => 9 passed in 0.11s (config_registry 3, settings_models 6). Confirmed test_config_registry.py reads the registry default DYNAMICALLY: it iterates CONFIG_DEFAULTS.items() and asserts resolve(key)==os.getenv(key,default) with default pulled from the registry itself (no hard-coded old defaults). So a collapsed key whose registry default changed still passes structurally; no test update was required."} (oracle byte-identical 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98).
Ask codex: "For each collapsed key, is the change byte-safe (env-set => env wins so runtime unchanged; or primary-path value preserved)? Did any collapse WEAKEN faithfulness (lower a threshold/evidence floor on a live path)? Are the DEFER classifications correct (genuinely behavior-changing / ambiguous left for the owner)? End with CONFIG-COLLAPSE-SAFE or CONFIG-COLLAPSE-REVISE (+ any key that must move to DEFER)."
Return verdict + points.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll run the config-collapse gate. Let me write the gate file and execute CODEX.

input.content:
# Config-Conflict Collapse Gate

You are a strict verification gate for a config refactor. 20 PG_ config keys had
CONFLICTING fallback default values across multiple call sites. We collapsed the
"safe" ones and DEFERRED the rest. Your job: confirm each collapse is byte-safe
(runtime behavior unchanged) and that faithfulness was NOT weakened.

## Rules of the domain
- BYTE-SAFE means: the key IS set in .env, so `os.getenv(KEY, fallback)` returns
  the env value at runtime REGARDLESS of the hardcoded fallback. Aligning the
  unused fallbacks to the env value is byte-identical (no runtime path reads the
  fallback).
- SAFE-PRIMARY means: key NOT in .env, but the multiple call sites still resolve
  to ONE effective value at runtime (either dead-code sites, or `'' or "default"`
  patterns). The primary/live-path value is kept; secondary aligned up; no
  faithfulness weakening.
- FAITHFULNESS RULE (absolute): a collapse must NOT lower a threshold or evidence
  floor on a LIVE runtime path. Lowering an evidence floor is allowed ONLY when
  .env explicitly sets the lower value (then runtime already uses it; aligning the
  fallback is byte-identical, not a behavior change).
- DEFER = genuinely behavior-changing or ambiguous; left for the owner.

## The 20 keys (investigation)
1. PG_AGENTIC_MAX_ROUNDS: seen [12,2]; env_set=15 -> chosen 15. BYTE-SAFE (env wins).
2. PG_ANALYSIS_BATCH_TIMEOUT: seen [180,240.0]; env=900.0 -> 900.0. BYTE-SAFE.
3. PG_ANALYSIS_PIPELINE: seen ['','8phase']; env NOT set -> '8phase'. SAFE-PRIMARY:
   react_agent.py:51 default='8phase'; line 674 uses '' then `or "8phase"` = '8phase'.
   The '' is not a real behavioral conflict. Collapse to '8phase'.
4. PG_CLUSTER_BATCH_TIMEOUT: seen [300,600]; env=600 -> 600. BYTE-SAFE.
5. PG_CONTENT_PER_SOURCE: seen [10000,25000]; env=25000 -> 25000. BYTE-SAFE.
6. PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD: seen [0.65,0.75]; env=0.75 -> 0.75.
   FAITHFULNESS-CRITICAL but env decides: .env=0.75 (the STRICTER). env wins; aligning
   defaults to 0.75 is byte-identical and raises the weak 0.65 path to strict.
7. PG_FETCH_CONCURRENCY: seen [10,5]; env=10 -> 10. BYTE-SAFE.
8. PG_FETCH_TIMEOUT: seen [20,30]; env NOT set -> 30. SAFE-PRIMARY:
   graph_v2.py:63 FETCH_TIMEOUT=20 is DEAD (module-scope, referenced nowhere;
   graph_v2 not a live pipeline). fetch_limiter.py:34 FETCH_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=30 is
   the LIVE path (used in rate_limited_fetch 82/87/94, exported via retrieval/__init__).
   Primary value=30.
9. PG_GENERATOR_MODEL: seen ['','<none>']; env='z-ai/glm-5.2' -> that. BYTE-SAFE.
   *** DEFERRED (only key deferred) ***
10. PG_MAX_CITATION_FREQUENCY: seen [5,8]; env=10 -> 10. BYTE-SAFE (runtime=10, neither
    seen default).
11. PG_MAX_CROSS_SOURCE_PAIRS: seen [200,50]; env=50 -> 50. BYTE-SAFE.
12. PG_MAX_TOTAL_ACADEMIC: seen [100,500]; env=100 -> 100. BYTE-SAFE.
13. PG_MAX_WORDS_PER_SECTION: seen [1500,2000]; env=3000 -> 3000. BYTE-SAFE (runtime=3000).
    Raising a MAX cap does not weaken any evidence/faithfulness floor.
14. PG_MIN_EVIDENCE_PER_SECTION: seen [3,5,8]; env=5 -> 5. FAITHFULNESS-CRITICAL but env
    decides: .env=5. env wins on every path; aligning all three fallbacks to 5 is
    byte-identical. Collapsing the 8-default path down to 5 reflects the already-
    authoritative runtime value (no behavior change).
15. PG_MIN_EVIDENCE_UTILIZATION: seen [0.30,0.40]; env=0.40 -> 0.40. FAITHFULNESS-CRITICAL
    but env decides: .env=0.40 (STRICTER). env wins; aligning is byte-identical, strengthens
    the weak 0.30 path.
16. PG_MIN_QUOTE_WORDS: seen [15,5]; env=5 -> 5. BYTE-SAFE. Quote-length knob, not an
    evidence/verification floor.
17. PG_MIN_TOTAL_WORDS: seen [0,10000]; env=0 -> 0. BYTE-SAFE. Report-length floor, not a
    faithfulness gate; env already disables it at runtime.
18. PG_REACT_INTERPRET_TIMEOUT: seen [240,180]; env NOT set -> 180. SAFE-PRIMARY:
    react_agent.py:48 _INTERPRET_TIMEOUT=240 is DEAD (referenced nowhere). The two LIVE
    reads are inline default=180 (line 3559 _write_interpretation 8phase write path; line
    8164 _interpret_results). Primary value=180.
19. PG_REDUNDANCY_JACCARD_THRESHOLD: seen [0.45,0.65]; env=0.65 -> 0.65. BYTE-SAFE. Dedup
    threshold, not a faithfulness floor.
20. PG_TARGET_TOTAL_WORDS: seen [12000,8000]; env=8000 -> 8000. BYTE-SAFE. Report-length
    target, not a faithfulness gate.

## Collapse result
keys_collapsed=19; keys_deferred=[PG_GENERATOR_MODEL]; files_changed=12;
collection_after: 16738 tests collected, 11 errors (matches baseline 16738/11 exactly).

## Verify result
oracle_matches=true; oracle_sha=9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98;
collection_ok=true; characterization_ok=true.
- Oracle byte-identical to golden on a clean sibling worktree (phase0):
  GOLDEN SHA-256 9c0a3d43...9db98 + ACCEPTANCE PASSED.
- The phase-cfg worktree's own replay fails with "3 recorded request(s) never replayed"
  but this is a HARNESS-VERSION artifact (phase-cfg's tracked oracle harness is older; it
  freezes only OpenRouterClient.generate, not generate_structured). PROVEN not the collapse:
  git-stashing the 12-file collapse diff -> replay fails IDENTICALLY at the committed
  byte-identical config-migration baseline. So the collapse working-tree change did NOT
  introduce it.
- Diff review: committed phase-cfg vs phase0 on the outline/retrieval path is pure
  `os.getenv("KEY","default")` -> `resolve("KEY")` substitution plus a settings import;
  the only non-getenv changed line is a `+import`. No control-flow/behavioral change.
- Characterization: pytest tests/test_config_registry.py tests/test_settings_models.py -q
  => 9 passed. test_config_registry reads the registry default DYNAMICALLY (iterates
  CONFIG_DEFAULTS.items(), asserts resolve(key)==os.getenv(key,default) with default from
  the registry itself), so a collapsed key whose registry default changed still passes.

## Your task
Answer precisely:
1. For each collapsed key, is the change byte-safe (env-set => env wins so runtime
   unchanged; OR primary-path value preserved with dead secondary)?
2. Did ANY collapse WEAKEN faithfulness (lower a threshold/evidence floor on a LIVE path)?
   Scrutinize the FAITHFULNESS-CRITICAL keys (6, 14, 15) and the MIN_* keys hardest.
3. Are the DEFER classifications correct (PG_GENERATOR_MODEL genuinely left for the owner)?
   Is there any key currently COLLAPSED that should instead be DEFERRED?

End your answer with exactly one of:
  CONFIG-COLLAPSE-SAFE
  CONFIG-COLLAPSE-REVISE (+ list any key that must move to DEFER)

content.content:
1	| `PG_ANALYSIS_PIPELINE` | SAFE-PRIMARY | Both old live expressions resolve to `8phase`; `'' or "8phase"` was not a behavioral alternative. |
2	| `PG_CLUSTER_BATCH_TIMEOUT` | BYTE-SAFE | Env `600` wins. |
3	| `PG_CONTENT_PER_SOURCE` | BYTE-SAFE | Env `25000` wins. |
4	| `PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD` | BYTE-SAFE | Runtime remains `0.75`; the weaker `0.65` fallback was never effective. |
5	| `PG_FETCH_CONCURRENCY` | BYTE-SAFE | Env `10` wins. |
6	| `PG_FETCH_TIMEOUT` | SAFE-PRIMARY | Live path remains `30`; only the unused/dead `20` value changed. |
7	| `PG_MAX_CITATION_FREQUENCY` | BYTE-SAFE | Env `10` wins. |
8	| `PG_MAX_CROSS_SOURCE_PAIRS` | BYTE-SAFE | Env `50` wins. |
9	| `PG_MAX_TOTAL_ACADEMIC` | BYTE-SAFE | Env `100` wins. |

content.text: CODEX returned its verdict: CONFIG-COLLAPSE-SAFE. I have the full output captured.

codex_points: Every collapsed key is byte-safe or safe-primary. BYTE-SAFE (env-set, env wins so runtime unchanged): PG_AGENTIC_MAX_ROUNDS, PG_ANALYSIS_BATCH_TIMEOUT, PG_CLUSTER_BATCH_TIMEOUT (600), PG_CONTENT_PER_SOURCE (25000), PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD (0.75), PG_FETCH_CONCURRENCY (10), PG_MAX_CITATION_FREQUENCY (10), PG_MAX_CROSS_SOURCE_PAIRS (50), PG_MAX_TOTAL_ACADEMIC (100), PG_MAX_WORDS_PER_SECTION (3000), PG_MIN_EVIDENCE_PER_SECTION (5), PG_MIN_EVIDENCE_UTILIZATION (0.40), PG_MIN_QUOTE_WORDS (5), PG_MIN_TOTAL_WORDS (0), PG_REDUNDANCY_JACCARD_THRESHOLD (0.65), PG_TARGET_TOTAL_WORDS (8000).

SAFE-PRIMARY (not in .env but live path value preserved, only dead/secondary changed): PG_ANALYSIS_PIPELINE (both old live expressions resolve to '8phase'; the '' or "8phase" was never a behavioral alternative), PG_FETCH_TIMEOUT (live path stays 30; only the dead/unused 20 constant changed), PG_REACT_INTERPRET_TIMEOUT (both live inline reads stay 180; only the unused 240 module constant changed).

No collapse weakened faithfulness on a live path. The three faithfulness-critical keys verified: NLI threshold 0.75 -> 0.75; minimum evidence count 5 -> 5 at runtime (the fallback 8 -> 5 edit is expressly permitted because .env already imposes 5); evidence utilization 0.40 -> 0.40. Other MIN_* keys are env-identical or are report/quote-length controls, not verification floors.

Deferring PG_GENERATOR_MODEL is valid and conservative: env dominance would make its collapse byte-safe too, but '' vs <none> have potentially different missing-env semantics, so leaving the owner decision untouched is reasonable. No currently collapsed key needs to move to DEFER.

Caveat on the verification method: the dynamic characterization test alone cannot validate the selected defaults because it reads them from the registry under test. The safety conclusion instead rests on env dominance, the live/dead-path analysis, and the byte-identical golden-oracle result (sha 9c0a3d43...9db98).
